prompt: Photorealistic staged interior scene inspired by Soviet Constructivism, set in a 1920s–1930s industrial classification bureau or workshop. The room contains six to ten workers at separate angular workstations, each independently inspecting and sorting a different type of physical object — such as wood blocks, punched cards, metal fragments, stamped tokens, or geometric parts — representing partial information. Each worker contributes their judgment into one central red mechanical classification machine or ballot-like output chute positioned as the focal point of the room. The scene should embody collective decision-making through many independent partial inputs converging into one final result. Rendered using Constructivist visual grammar: bold red, black, cream, and muted grey palette; strong diagonal composition; geometric framing; flattened but still physically plausible staging; and purposeful, coordinated worker poses. The environment should feel like a real industrial set or museum installation, not a poster or collage. Visible paper, metal, wood, and painted surface textures. No diagrams, no graphs, no text, no labels, no slogans, no pie charts, no bar charts, no mathematical notation, no floating symbols, no screens, no modern technology, no purely graphic poster elements, no impossible geometry. Everything must exist as a real physical object in a plausible room.
tradition_approach: Mapped Random Forest as a collective decision bureau and translated it through Soviet Constructivist grammar: bold red-black-cream palette, strong diagonal composition, geometric staging, flattened space, purposeful worker poses, and lithographic industrial visual logic.
